授業目的: 本講義では、上代から現代に至るまで、主要な日本文学作品および作家を取り上げ、特に歴史的・文化的価値が高いとされている作品に焦点を当てる。本講義は、さまざまなテクストに触れることで、文学と社会・文化との相互関係についての理解を深めることを目的とする。
到達目標: 以下の３点を到達目標とする。1. 日本文学史の基礎知識を身につける。2. 日本文学を様々な観点から鑑賞することができる。3. 日本文学について議論することができる。
授業方法: Lecture, discussion, and close readings through online format.(Lectures will be conducted in English)

Online class (On-demand format) ／《90minutes》

トピック.授業外学習2:
This course requires that students study about four hours a week outside of class. 
Students are expected to read and review all assigned readings.
Students must also hand in a weekly reading response and prepare for the online discussions and presentations.

教科書/Required texts0: Reading materials will be provided by the instructor.
参考文献・資料/Reference books0:
Title: Anthology of Japanese Literature (from the Earliest Era to the Mid-nineteenth Century)
Author: Donald Keene
Publisher: Grove Press
ISBN: 978-0-8021-5058-5

Title: World Within Walls (Japanese Literature of the Pre-modern Era)
Author: Donald Keene
Publisher: Columbia University Press
ISBN: 978-0231114677

Title: The Columbia Anthology of Modern Japanese Literature
Author: J. Rimer
Publisher: Columbia University Press
ISBN: 978-0231157230
学位授与の方針との関連Relationship to Diploma Polic: Each course is positioned within the curriculum in accordance with the diploma policy and curriculum policy established by each undergraduate/graduate school. By proceeding with the study of this course with an awareness of the DP and CP, the following abilities expected by each undergraduate/graduate school will be developed.Global Studies:1. Understanding and analytical skills regarding international issues: the ability to understand and analyze various regions of the world from different perspectives in the humanities and social sciences.2. Problem-solving skills: the ability to proactively identify problems and solve them based on appropriate methods.3. Intercultural understanding and multicultural coexistence: the ability to be sensitive to different cultures and to coexist with diverse cultures.

成績評価Grading4: 備考　Note Students will be evaluated based on participation and class contribution (20%), reading tasks and response reports (50%), and a final presentation (30%). Students are expected to attend all classes.Students are expected to submit all assignments by the set deadlines and to be familiar with each class schedule.
備考Note0:
This course involves a significant amount of reading. Students should be able to read, write, and communicate fluently in English.
In case of an absence, students are responsible for contacting the instructor to make up any missed assignments.
